event: Admitted with body mass index of 67 kg/m2 and multiple comorbidities
Underwent Da Vinci-assisted, laparoscopic, one-stage, single-anastomosis DS procedure
Terminal ileum was identified and ran 250 cm proximally up the ileum
Greater omentum was taken from the greater curvature distally 3 cm from the pylorus
Sleeve gastrectomy was done loosely over a 34 French bougie-sized Edlich tube
Staple line was oversewn
Duodenum was transected 3 cm from the pylorus
Duodenal stump was oversewn with a running absorbable suture
Two-layer hand sewn anastomosis between the duodenum and the ileum was created at the 250 cm mark
An upper gastrointestinal study (UGI) on postoperative day (POD) 2 was negative for a leak
Bariatric phase 1 diet was started and the patient tolerated it well
Discharged on POD 3
Developed persistent nausea, fatigue, and severe abdominal pain on POD 11
Tachycardic and anemic with a leukocytosis
Abdominal exam revealed significant distension with guarding
UGI was negative
Computed tomography (CT) of the abdomen showing free fluid suggestive of a leak or bleeding
Hemoperitoneum was evacuated, but active bleeding source was not identifiable
Three large 19F Blake drains were placed
Transfused with 2 units of packed red blood cells (PRBCs) intra-operatively
Sent to intensive care unit (ICU) for further monitoring
Doing well, and transferred to step down
Became hypotensive, unresponsive to fluids, and her abdominal drain had increased sanguinous output
Second drain showed frank bile which was confirmed with elevated bilirubin and amylase
Tachycardic
Taken back to the OR for exploratory laparotomy with the presumptive diagnosis of bleeding with duodenal stump blowout
Laparotomy through a generous right subcostal incision
Large amount of hemoperitoneum was encountered
Packing identified as coming from the gastroduodenal artery which was over-sewn
Duodenal stump was found to have total stump blow and the staple line was completely undone
Malecot tube was placed in the duodenum and over-sewn with a purse-string suture
Blood loss was 2500 cc and the patient received 5 units of PRBCs and 4 units of fresh frozen plasma intra-operatively
Transferred to the ICU
Developed hemorrhagic fluid collections seen on CT scan and atelectasis
Developed acute thrombosis of the left internal jugular and subclavian veins at a previous central catheter site without pulmonary embolism
Placed on therapeutic Lovenox and octreotide
Given intravenous piperacillin/taxobactam which was later changed to cefazolin
Discharged on POD 17 to her home with home health care for dressing changes and total parenteral nutrition
